file.content:
"""MLflow tracking and tracing utilities for OpenAI agent calls.

Provides:
- ``configure()``: one-time setup that enables experiment tracking AND
  auto-tracing via ``mlflow.openai.autolog()``. Auto-tracing captures every
  OpenAI / Agents SDK call as a trace span (tokens, latency, model I/O) with
  zero per-call instrumentation.
- ``agent_run()``: context manager that wraps an agent invocation with an
  MLflow *run* (structured params/metrics). Degrades gracefully to a no-op
  when MLflow is disabled or the tracking server is unreachable.
- ``trace_span()``: lightweight context manager for manual parent spans.
  Wrap a block that makes one or more OpenAI calls to group the auto-logged
  child spans under a descriptive parent span in the Traces UI.

Usage::

    # Experiment tracking (params + metrics)
    with agent_run("clinical_discussion", {"model": "gpt-4o", "concern": name}) as metrics:
        result = await Runner.run(agent, message)
        metrics["output_chars"] = len(result.final_output)
    # latency_ms is recorded automatically on exit

    # Manual parent span (trace view)
    with trace_span("generate_discussion", {"concern": name, "num_snps": n}):
        result = await Runner.run(agent, message)
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import os
import time
from contextlib import contextmanager
from typing import Any, Generator

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

_enabled: bool = False
_tracing_enabled: bool = False


def configure(
    *,
    enabled: bool,
    tracking_uri: str,
    experiment_name: str,
    username: str = "",
    password: str = "",
    tracing_enabled: bool = True,
) -> None:
    """Configure MLflow tracking and tracing. Called once at application startup.

    Controlled by two independent flags:

    - ``enabled`` (env: ``MLFLOW_ENABLED``): master switch for all MLflow
      features. When ``False``, nothing is initialised.
    - ``tracing_enabled`` (env: ``MLFLOW_TRACING_ENABLED``): controls
      distributed tracing only (``mlflow.openai.autolog()`` and
      ``trace_span()``).  Set to ``False`` to keep experiment tracking
      (runs / params / metrics) while skipping trace collection.

    When ``username`` and ``password`` are provided they are written to the
    ``MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME`` / ``MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD`` environment
    variables that the MLflow client reads for HTTP basic authentication.

    Args:
        enabled: Master switch — whether to enable any MLflow feature.
        tracking_uri: MLflow tracking server URI.
        experiment_name: Name of the MLflow experiment to log runs under.
        username: Basic auth username for the tracking server.
        password: Basic auth password for the tracking server.
        tracing_enabled: Whether to enable distributed tracing via
            ``mlflow.openai.autolog()`` and ``trace_span()``.
    """
    global _enabled, _tracing_enabled
    _enabled = enabled
    _tracing_enabled = enabled and tracing_enabled

    if not enabled:
        logger.debug("[mlflow] Tracking disabled")
        return

    try:
        import mlflow

        if username:
            os.environ["MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME"] = username
        if password:
            os.environ["MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD"] = password

        mlflow.set_tracking_uri(tracking_uri)
        mlflow.set_experiment(experiment_name)

        if tracing_enabled:
            # Non-blocking async trace I/O — traces are flushed in a background
            # thread so agent streaming is not delayed by network round-trips.
            os.environ.setdefault("MLFLOW_ENABLE_ASYNC_TRACE_LOGGING", "true")

            # Auto-trace all OpenAI / Agents SDK calls: captures model, tokens,
            # latency, and full prompt/completion I/O as nested spans in the
            # MLflow Traces UI.  Works with Runner.run(), Runner.run_streamed(),
            # and direct openai.Client calls.
            mlflow.openai.autolog()

        logger.debug(
            "[mlflow] Tracking configured",
            extra={
                "tracking_uri": tracking_uri,
                "experiment": experiment_name,
                "auth": "basic" if username else "none",
                "tracing": tracing_enabled,
            },
        )
    except Exception:
        _enabled = False
        _tracing_enabled = False
        logger.warning("[mlflow] Failed to configure tracking — tracking disabled", exc_info=True)


@contextmanager
def agent_run(
    run_name: str,
    params: dict[str, Any],
    tags: dict[str, str] | None = None,
) -> Generator[dict[str, float], None, None]:
    """Context manager for tracking an OpenAI agent run with MLflow.

    Logs ``params`` when the run starts and automatically records
    ``latency_ms`` on exit. The caller may add extra metrics to the
    yielded ``metrics`` dict before the context exits.

    When tracking is disabled or the tracking server is unreachable, this
    is a silent no-op — the wrapped agent call proceeds normally.

    Args:
        run_name: Human-readable name for the MLflow run.
        params: Key/value pairs to log as MLflow parameters.
        tags: Optional key/value string tags for the run.

    Yields:
        A mutable ``metrics`` dict. Add float values to be logged on exit.
        ``latency_ms`` is set automatically and must not be overwritten.

    Example::

        with agent_run("clinical_chat", {"model": "gpt-4o"}) as metrics:
            result = await Runner.run(agent, prompt)
            metrics["output_chars"] = len(result.final_output)
    """
    metrics: dict[str, float] = {}
    start = time.perf_counter()

    if not _enabled:
        yield metrics
        return

    import mlflow  # deferred — only imported when tracking is active

    tracking_active = False
    try:
        mlflow.start_run(run_name=run_name, tags=tags or {})
        mlflow.log_params({k: str(v)[:250] for k, v in params.items()})
        tracking_active = True
    except Exception:
        logger.warning("[mlflow] Could not start run '%s'", run_name, exc_info=True)

    try:
        yield metrics
    finally:
        duration_ms = round((time.perf_counter() - start) * 1000, 2)
        metrics.setdefault("latency_ms", duration_ms)

        if tracking_active:
            try:
                mlflow.log_metrics(metrics)
            except Exception:
                logger.debug("[mlflow] Could not log metrics for run '%s'", run_name)
            finally:
                try:
                    mlflow.end_run()
                except Exception:
                    logger.debug("[mlflow] Could not end run '%s'", run_name)


@contextmanager
def trace_span(
    name: str,
    attributes: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
) -> Generator[None, None, None]:
    """Context manager that creates a named MLflow trace span.

    Use this to create a parent span that groups the auto-logged child spans
    from ``mlflow.openai.autolog()`` under a descriptive label in the Traces
    UI.  When tracking is disabled or the server is unreachable, this is a
    silent no-op.

    Args:
        name: Span name shown in the MLflow Traces UI
              (e.g., ``"run_concerns_stream"``).
        attributes: Optional key/value pairs attached to the span as metadata
                    (e.g., ``{"concern": "Cardiac", "num_snps": 5}``).

    Example::

        with trace_span("run_concerns_stream", {"num_messages": len(messages)}):
            result = Runner.run_streamed(agent, input=messages)
            async for event in result.stream_events():
                ...
    """
    if not _tracing_enabled:
        yield
        return

    import mlflow  # deferred — only imported when tracking is active

    try:
        with mlflow.start_span(name=name) as span:
            if attributes:
                span.set_attributes({k: str(v) for k, v in attributes.items()})
            yield
    except Exception:
        logger.debug("[mlflow] Could not create trace span '%s'", name)
        yield

content.text:
Here's what you currently have and what you can add next:

---

### What you have now
| Feature | Status |
|---|---|
| Params per run (`model`, `num_snps`, etc.) | ✅ |
| Metrics per run (`latency_ms`, `output_chars`) | ✅ |
| Auto-tracing (OpenAI spans with token counts) | ✅ |
| Manual parent spans (`trace_span`) | ✅ |

---

### What you can add — ranked by value for your use case

**1. Tags** *(easiest, high value)*
Classify runs so you can filter in the UI. For example:
```
agent: concerns_extractor
environment: production
app_version: 1.0.0
```
Currently `agent_run()` accepts a `tags` dict but nothing passes it. You'd add tags like `{"agent": "concerns_extractor", "env": "production"}` to each call.

---

**2. Parent / child runs** *(medium effort, high value)*
Right now every concern discussion creates its own independent run. If a patient session generates 5 concerns, you see 5 disconnected runs. With parent/child runs you'd see:

```
📁 clinical_session  (parent)
   ├── clinical_discussion/Cardiac
   ├── clinical_discussion/Diabetes Type 2
   └── clinical_discussion/Inflammation
```

---

**3. Artifacts** *(medium effort, useful for debugging)*
Log the final agent output as a `.txt` or `.json` file attached to the run. In the UI you can click the run and read the exact text the agent produced.

---

**4. Token usage metrics** *(medium effort, high value for cost tracking)*
The autolog captures token counts in traces, but not in runs/metrics. You can extract `prompt_tokens`, `completion_tokens`, `total_tokens` from the OpenAI response and log them as metrics — then chart cost trends over time.

---

**Which of these do you want to implement first?**

---

## Implementation Plan: 4 MLflow Features

### Preliminary observations

Before listing per-file changes it is worth naming the structural facts that drive every decision below.

- `mlflow_tracker.py` is the single source-of-truth module; all new helpers live there.
- The three agent files are consumers; they import only `agent_run` and `trace_span` today.
- `agent_run()` is a synchronous `@contextmanager`. It is used inside `async def` functions via `with`, which is correct in Python (sync context managers are fine inside coroutines). This pattern must be preserved for every new helper.
- `stream_all_discussions` and `run_concerns_stream` and `run_clinical_chat_stream` are all `async def` generators (`AsyncGenerator`). A sync context manager wrapping the outer loop body is legal; a sync context manager wrapping the loop itself (as a parent run) needs careful placement so it does not interact with `yield` points inside — this is the critical edge case for Feature 2.
- `asyncio.gather` in `generate_all_discussions` spawns all concern tasks in parallel, so each child `agent_run` call runs in its own coroutine. With MLflow's thread-local active-run tracking, a parent run started in the dispatcher coroutine will NOT be visible as the active run inside the child coroutines. This is the most important edge case for Feature 2.
- `RunResultStreaming.raw_responses` is populated only after `stream_events()` is exhausted. Both streaming sites already drain the iterator fully before exiting the `with agent_run(...)` block, so Feature 4 (token usage) can safely read it just after the `async for` loop closes.

---

### Feature 1 — Tags

#### `mlflow_tracker.py`

1. Add a module-level global `_default_tags: dict[str, str] = {}` alongside `_enabled` and `_tracing_enabled`.

2. Add `default_tags: dict[str, str] | None = None` as a keyword parameter to `configure()`, after `tracing_enabled`. Store it with `global _default_tags` then assign `_default_tags = default_tags or {}` inside the `if not enabled` guard (so the value is always reset even when disabled) — or more precisely, assign it before the `if not enabled` return so it is captured regardless of the enabled state.

3. At the end of the `try` block inside `configure()`, after `mlflow.set_experiment(experiment_name)` and before the `if tracing_enabled:` branch, call `mlflow.set_experiment_tags(_default_tags)` — but only when `_default_tags` is non-empty, to avoid a no-op network call.

4. In `agent_run()`, replace the current `mlflow.start_run(run_name=run_name, tags=tags or {})` call with a merged dict: `{**_default_tags, **(tags or {})}`. Call-level tags win over default tags because they appear second in the merge.

#### `main.py`

5. In the `configure_mlflow(...)` call inside `lifespan()`, add the keyword argument `default_tags={"environment": settings.general.environment.value}`. `settings.general.environment` is an `Environment(str, Enum)` so `.value` gives the plain string (`"development"`, `"staging"`, `"production"`).

#### `agent_clinical_discussion.py`

6. In `generate_discussion_for_concern`, add `tags={"agent": "clinical_discussion"}` to the `agent_run(...)` call (lines 405-415).

7. In `stream_all_discussions`, add `tags={"agent": "clinical_discussion_stream"}` to the `agent_run(...)` call (lines 565-577).

#### `agent_extract_concern.py`

8. In `run_concerns_stream`, add `tags={"agent": "concerns_extractor"}` to the `agent_run(...)` call (lines 383-392).

#### `agent_clinical_chat.py`

9. In `run_clinical_chat_stream`, add `tags={"agent": "clinical_chat"}` to the `agent_run(...)` call (lines 423-434).

---

### Feature 2 — Parent/child runs

#### `mlflow_tracker.py`

1. Add a new exported `@contextmanager` function `session_run(run_name: str, tags: dict[str, str] | None = None) -> Generator[None, None, None]`. Its body mirrors `agent_run` but: no `params` or `metrics` dict is yielded; it calls `mlflow.start_run(run_name=run_name, tags={**_default_tags, **(tags or {})})` without `nested=True` (it always creates a top-level run); it calls `mlflow.end_run()` in the `finally` block. It is a pure parent container — no metrics logging, no latency recording.

2. In `agent_run()`, change the `mlflow.start_run(...)` line. Before calling it, check `mlflow.active_run()`. If an active run is detected, pass `nested=True`. If no active run exists, pass nothing (or explicitly `nested=False`). This is a one-line conditional: `nested = mlflow.active_run() is not None` then `mlflow.start_run(run_name=run_name, tags=merged_tags, nested=nested)`.

3. Update the module docstring and the `__all__`-equivalent export comment to include `session_run`.

#### `agent_clinical_discussion.py`

4. Import `session_run` from `mlflow_tracker` (add to the existing import line on line 28).

5. In `generate_all_discussions` (lines 429-477): wrap the body of the function — specifically the `tasks = [...]` and `await asyncio.gather(*tasks)` block — with `with session_run("generate_all_discussions"):`. However, read the critical edge case below first.

   **Critical edge case — asyncio.gather + nested runs.** MLflow tracks the active run in a thread-local (actually a Python context variable in newer versions, but effective behavior can differ by MLflow version). When `asyncio.gather` fans out to multiple coroutines, each coroutine runs in the same event-loop thread but in separate asyncio `Task` objects that inherit the context at task creation time. The parent `session_run` is entered before `asyncio.gather`, so the active run IS visible in child tasks if MLflow uses `contextvars.copy_context()` (which asyncio does by default in Python 3.7+). Test this assumption before relying on it. If the context is not propagated, the child `agent_run` calls will see no active run and will start top-level runs rather than nested ones.

   The safe, environment-agnostic approach: do not rely on context propagation for gather. Instead, pass the parent run ID explicitly. But since the existing `agent_run` API does not accept a parent run ID, the simpler alternative is to wrap `generate_all_discussions` with `session_run` and accept that nested detection depends on asyncio context propagation — add a note in the code to verify behavior in the MLflow version used.

6. In `stream_all_discussions` (lines 498-612): this is an `async def` that `yield`s inside a `for` loop. Wrapping the entire `for` loop with `with session_run(...)` is valid Python — a sync context manager CAN wrap a body that contains `yield` statements in an `async def` generator. Specifically, place `with session_run("stream_all_discussions"):` around the `for idx, concern in enumerate(active_concerns):` loop. The `agent_run` inside the loop body will then detect an active run and correctly pass `nested=True`.

   **Critical edge case — async generator + sync context manager.** If the caller stops iterating early (e.g., the HTTP connection drops), Python will `.athrow(GeneratorExit)` into the generator. The `with session_run(...)` block's `__exit__` will be called because `contextmanager`'s `GeneratorExit` handling propagates the exit. This means MLflow `end_run()` is called correctly on early termination. No special handling is needed, but ensure the `finally` block in `session_run` unconditionally calls `end_run()`.

#### Existing agent files — no other changes needed for Feature 2

`run_concerns_stream` and `run_clinical_chat_stream` are single-agent calls with no looping over multiple agent invocations, so they do not need a parent `session_run`. The `agent_run` inside each will simply start as a top-level run (no active run at that point), which is the correct behavior.

---

### Feature 3 — Artifacts

#### `mlflow_tracker.py`

1. Add `log_text(text: str, filename: str) -> None`. Implementation:
   - Guard: `if not _enabled: return`.
   - `import mlflow` deferred.
   - Call `mlflow.log_text(text, filename)` inside a `try/except Exception` that logs a debug warning on failure.
   - No return value; graceful no-op on any failure.

2. Add `log_dict(data: dict[str, Any], filename: str) -> None`. Implementation:
   - Guard: `if not _enabled: return`.
   - `import mlflow` deferred.
   - Call `mlflow.log_dict(data, filename)` inside a `try/except Exception`.
   - Same graceful no-op pattern.

3. Add both to the imports in the consumers (see below).

#### `agent_clinical_discussion.py`

4. Add `log_text, log_dict` to the `from app.ai.mlflow_tracker import ...` line.

5. In `generate_discussion_for_concern`, after `discussion = _post_process_discussion(...)` and before `metrics["output_chars"] = ...`, add: `log_text(discussion, f"discussion_{concern_name.replace(' ', '_')}.txt")`. This must be inside the `with agent_run(...)` block so an active run exists when `mlflow.log_text` is called.

6. In `stream_all_discussions`, inside the `with agent_run(...)` block, after the `async for event` loop completes and `final_text` is assigned (after line 593), add: `log_text(final_text, f"discussion_stream_{concern_name.replace(' ', '_')}.txt")`. This must also be inside the `agent_run` block, before it exits.

#### `agent_extract_concern.py`

7. Add `log_dict` to the `from app.ai.mlflow_tracker import ...` line.

8. In `run_concerns_stream`, after `concerns = parse_concerns_from_items(result)` (line 407), if `concerns` is not None, call `log_dict(concerns.model_dump(), "extracted_concerns.json")`. This must be inside the `with agent_run(...)` block.

#### `agent_clinical_chat.py`

9. Add `log_text` to the `from app.ai.mlflow_tracker import ...` line.

10. In `run_clinical_chat_stream`, after `final_text = _post_process_response(...)` (line 449) and before `metrics["output_chars"] = ...`, add: `log_text(final_text, "clinical_chat_response.txt")`. This must be inside the `with agent_run(...)` block (which it is — the metrics assignment is already inside).

---

### Feature 4 — Token usage metrics

#### `mlflow_tracker.py`

1. Add `log_token_usage(result: Any) -> None`. Implementation:
   - Guard: `if not _enabled: return`.
   - `import mlflow` deferred.
   - Access `raw_responses = getattr(result, "raw_responses", None)`. If `None` or empty, return early (graceful no-op).
   - Iterate: `for resp in raw_responses`, access `usage = getattr(resp, "usage", None)`. Accumulate `input_tokens += getattr(usage, "input_tokens", 0) or 0` and `output_tokens += getattr(usage, "output_tokens", 0) or 0`.
   - Call `mlflow.log_metrics({"input_tokens": float(input_tokens), "output_tokens": float(output_tokens), "total_tokens": float(input_tokens + output_tokens)})` inside `try/except Exception`.
   - The function must be called while an active run is open. Document this requirement in the docstring.

2. Add `log_token_usage` to the exports.

#### `agent_clinical_discussion.py`

3. Add `log_token_usage` to the `from app.ai.mlflow_tracker import ...` line.

4. In `generate_discussion_for_concern`, inside `with agent_run(...)`, after `result = await Runner.run(agent, user_message)` (line 417) and before `_post_process_discussion(...)`, add: `log_token_usage(result)`.

5. In `stream_all_discussions`, the streaming path is `result = Runner.run_streamed(...)` followed by `async for event in result.stream_events(): ...`. The `result` object here is a `RunResultStreaming` instance. After the `async for` loop finishes (line 592, after all events are consumed), `result.raw_responses` is populated. Add `log_token_usage(result)` immediately after the `async for` loop, before `raw_text = "".join(accumulated)`. This is still inside the `with agent_run(...)` block — the block does not exit until after the streaming code section.

#### `agent_extract_concern.py`

6. Add `log_token_usage` to the `from app.ai.mlflow_tracker import ...` line.

7. In `run_concerns_stream`, `result = Runner.run_streamed(...)` is on line 394 and the `async for event in result.stream_events()` loop ends around line 402. After the loop closes (around line 404 where `metrics["output_chars"]` is assigned), add `log_token_usage(result)`. This is inside the `with agent_run(...)` block.

#### `agent_clinical_chat.py`

8. Add `log_token_usage` to the `from app.ai.mlflow_tracker import ...` line.

9. In `run_clinical_chat_stream`, `result = Runner.run_streamed(...)` is on line 436 and the `async for event` loop ends around line 445. After the loop, add `log_token_usage(result)` before `raw_text = "".join(accumulated)`. This is inside the `with agent_run(...)` block.

---

### Ordering and dependency issues

The four features have the following dependency graph:

- **Feature 1 (Tags)** has no dependencies. It can be implemented standalone. However, every subsequent feature that modifies `agent_run` calls in the agent files should carry the `tags=` argument already added in Feature 1, so do Feature 1 first to avoid touching those lines twice.

- **Feature 2 (Parent/child runs)** depends on Feature 1 being done because the merged tags logic in `agent_run` and `session_run` should be consistent. Also depends on `session_run` not existing yet — implement it only in Feature 2.

- **Feature 3 (Artifacts)** has no dependency on Feature 2. It only requires an active run, which `agent_run` already provides. However, if Feature 2 is done first, the artifact log calls in `stream_all_discussions` must be placed inside the child `agent_run` block (not the parent `session_run`), because `mlflow.log_text` logs to the currently active innermost run. This is correct regardless — the artifact belongs to the per-concern run, not the session run.

- **Feature 4 (Token usage)** is fully independent of Features 2 and 3. It only requires an active run.

**Recommended implementation order:** 1 → 2 → 3 → 4. Features 3 and 4 can be done in parallel (they touch different lines in each file).

---

### Full edge-case catalogue

**asyncio.gather + context propagation (Feature 2)**
Python asyncio copies the `contextvars` context when creating a task. MLflow 2.x uses `contextvars` to store the active run. Therefore `asyncio.gather`-spawned tasks created inside a `session_run` block should inherit the active run context and child `agent_run` calls should correctly detect `mlflow.active_run() is not None`. This should be verified against the actual MLflow version in use. If it does not propagate, child runs will silently start as top-level runs rather than nested ones — no error, just missing hierarchy in the UI.

**async generator + sync context manager (Feature 2 and 3)**
`stream_all_discussions` is `async def` and yields inside a sync `with` block. This is valid Python. The generator's cleanup path (through `GeneratorExit` on early disconnect) correctly triggers `__exit__` on the sync context manager. MLflow `end_run()` is guaranteed to be called via the `finally` in `session_run`.

**Streaming result usage availability (Feature 4)**
`Runner.run_streamed()` returns a `RunResultStreaming` object immediately. The `raw_responses` attribute is only fully populated after `stream_events()` is exhausted. In all three streaming call sites (`stream_all_discussions`, `run_concerns_stream`, `run_clinical_chat_stream`), the code already fully drains `stream_events()` before exiting the `with agent_run(...)` block. Therefore `log_token_usage(result)` placed immediately after the `async for` loop is always safe.

**Artifact logging outside an active run (Feature 3)**
`mlflow.log_text` and `mlflow.log_dict` raise or silently fail if called outside an active run. The `log_text`/`log_dict` helpers defined in `mlflow_tracker.py` must wrap the call in `try/except` for graceful degradation. Additionally, all call sites in the agent files are deliberately placed inside their respective `with agent_run(...)` blocks — this must not be broken during refactoring.

**`mlflow.set_experiment_tags` call timing (Feature 1)**
`mlflow.set_experiment_tags()` is called in `configure()` at startup, which is before any run exists. This is the correct MLflow API for attaching tags to the experiment object itself (visible in the Experiments UI). It is distinct from run-level tags passed to `mlflow.start_run()`. Both are needed; the experiment-level call in `configure()` provides persistent metadata, and the run-level merge in `agent_run` provides per-run filtering.

**`default_tags` assignment when disabled (Feature 1)**
The `_default_tags` global must be assigned before the `if not enabled: return` guard in `configure()`, otherwise a subsequent call with `enabled=True` after a disabled call would carry stale tags. The correct placement is: `global _default_tags; _default_tags = default_tags or {}` at the top of the function body, before the guard.

**`session_run` and `agent_run` both calling `mlflow.end_run()` (Feature 2)**
MLflow's `end_run()` ends the innermost active run. When `session_run` is the parent and `agent_run` is a nested child, the child's `agent_run.__exit__` calls `end_run()` which ends the child. The parent `session_run.__exit__` subsequently calls `end_run()` which ends the parent. This is the correct MLflow API sequence. No interference occurs.

**Filename sanitisation for artifact logging (Feature 3)**
Concern names like `"Diabetes Type 2"` or `"SNPs Not To Miss"` contain spaces and special characters. The plan uses `.replace(' ', '_')` in the filename. This should be extended to also replace or remove any character that is invalid in a file path segment. A simple approach: `re.sub(r'[^\w\-]', '_', concern_name)` — this can be done inline at the call site or extracted into a tiny private helper `_safe_filename(name: str) -> str` in `mlflow_tracker.py` if the pattern appears more than twice.

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `/opt/development/git/shokworks/intellxxdna/backend/intell-lexxi-backend/app/ai/mlflow_tracker.py`
- `/opt/development/git/shokworks/intellxxdna/backend/intell-lexxi-backend/app/ai/agent_clinical_discussion.py`
- `/opt/development/git/shokworks/intellxxdna/backend/intell-lexxi-backend/app/ai/agent_extract_concern.py`
- `/opt/development/git/shokworks/intellxxdna/backend/intell-lexxi-backend/app/ai/agent_clinical_chat.py`
- `/opt/development/git/shokworks/intellxxdna/backend/intell-lexxi-backend/app/main.py`

`run_concerns_stream` and `run_clinical_chat_stream` are single-agent calls with no looping over multiple agent invocations, so they do not need a parent `session_run`. The `agent_run` inside each will simply start as a top-level run (no active run at that point), which is the correct behavior.

---

### Ordering and dependency issues

The four features have the following dependency graph:

- **Feature 1 (Tags)** has no dependencies. It can be implemented standalone. However, every subsequent feature that modifies `agent_run` calls in the agent files should carry the `tags=` argument already added in Feature 1, so do Feature 1 first to avoid touching those lines twice.

- **Feature 2 (Parent/child runs)** depends on Feature 1 being done because the merged tags logic in `agent_run` and `session_run` should be consistent. Also depends on `session_run` not existing yet — implement it only in Feature 2.

- **Feature 3 (Artifacts)** has no dependency on Feature 2. It only requires an active run, which `agent_run` already provides. However, if Feature 2 is done first, the artifact log calls in `stream_all_discussions` must be placed inside the child `agent_run` block (not the parent `session_run`), because `mlflow.log_text` logs to the currently active innermost run. This is correct regardless — the artifact belongs to the per-concern run, not the session run.

- **Feature 4 (Token usage)** is fully independent of Features 2 and 3. It only requires an active run.

**Recommended implementation order:** 1 → 2 → 3 → 4. Features 3 and 4 can be done in parallel (they touch different lines in each file).

---

### Full edge-case catalogue

**asyncio.gather + context propagation (Feature 2)**
Python asyncio copies the `contextvars` context when creating a task. MLflow 2.x uses `contextvars` to store the active run. Therefore `asyncio.gather`-spawned tasks created inside a `session_run` block should inherit the active run context and child `agent_run` calls should correctly detect `mlflow.active_run() is not None`. This should be verified against the actual MLflow version in use. If it does not propagate, child runs will silently start as top-level runs rather than nested ones — no error, just missing hierarchy in the UI.

**async generator + sync context manager (Feature 2 and 3)**
`stream_all_discussions` is `async def` and yields inside a sync `with` block. This is valid Python. The generator's cleanup path (through `GeneratorExit` on early disconnect) correctly triggers `__exit__` on the sync context manager. MLflow `end_run()` is guaranteed to be called via the `finally` in `session_run`.

**Streaming result usage availability (Feature 4)**
`Runner.run_streamed()` returns a `RunResultStreaming` object immediately. The `raw_responses` attribute is only fully populated after `stream_events()` is exhausted. In all three streaming call sites (`stream_all_discussions`, `run_concerns_stream`, `run_clinical_chat_stream`), the code already fully drains `stream_events()` before exiting the `with agent_run(...)` block. Therefore `log_token_usage(result)` placed immediately after the `async for` loop is always safe.

**Artifact logging outside an active run (Feature 3)**
`mlflow.log_text` and `mlflow.log_dict` raise or silently fail if called outside an active run. The `log_text`/`log_dict` helpers defined in `mlflow_tracker.py` must wrap the call in `try/except` for graceful degradation. Additionally, all call sites in the agent files are deliberately placed inside their respective `with agent_run(...)` blocks — this must not be broken during refactoring.

**`mlflow.set_experiment_tags` call timing (Feature 1)**
`mlflow.set_experiment_tags()` is called in `configure()` at startup, which is before any run exists. This is the correct MLflow API for attaching tags to the experiment object itself (visible in the Experiments UI). It is distinct from run-level tags passed to `mlflow.start_run()`. Both are needed; the experiment-level call in `configure()` provides persistent metadata, and the run-level merge in `agent_run` provides per-run filtering.

**`default_tags` assignment when disabled (Feature 1)**
The `_default_tags` global must be assigned before the `if not enabled: return` guard in `configure()`, otherwise a subsequent call with `enabled=True` after a disabled call would carry stale tags. The correct placement is: `global _default_tags; _default_tags = default_tags or {}` at the top of the function body, before the guard.

**`session_run` and `agent_run` both calling `mlflow.end_run()` (Feature 2)**
MLflow's `end_run()` ends the innermost active run. When `session_run` is the parent and `agent_run` is a nested child, the child's `agent_run.__exit__` calls `end_run()` which ends the child. The parent `session_run.__exit__` subsequently calls `end_run()` which ends the parent. This is the correct MLflow API sequence. No interference occurs.

**Filename sanitisation for artifact logging (Feature 3)**
Concern names like `"Diabetes Type 2"` or `"SNPs Not To Miss"` contain spaces and special characters. The plan uses `.replace(' ', '_')` in the filename. This should be extended to also replace or remove any character that is invalid in a file path segment. A simple approach: `re.sub(r'[^\w\-]', '_', concern_name)` — this can be done inline at the call site or extracted into a tiny private helper `_safe_filename(name: str) -> str` in `mlflow_tracker.py` if the pattern appears more than twice.

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `/opt/development/git/shokworks/intellxxdna/backend/intell-lexxi-backend/app/ai/mlflow_tracker.py`
- `/opt/development/git/shokworks/intellxxdna/backend/intell-lexxi-backend/app/ai/agent_clinical_discussion.py`

input.content:
"""MLflow tracking and tracing utilities for OpenAI agent calls.

Provides:
- ``configure()``: one-time setup that enables experiment tracking AND
  auto-tracing via ``mlflow.openai.autolog()``. Auto-tracing captures every
  OpenAI / Agents SDK call as a trace span (tokens, latency, model I/O) with
  zero per-call instrumentation.
- ``session_run()``: context manager that opens a *parent* MLflow run. Use
  this to group all concern discussions from one patient session under a
  single collapsible entry in the Experiments UI.
- ``agent_run()``: context manager that wraps one agent invocation with an
  MLflow *child* run (params + metrics). Automatically nests under an active
  ``session_run``; starts as a top-level run when used standalone. Degrades
  gracefully to a no-op when MLflow is disabled or unreachable.
- ``trace_span()``: lightweight context manager for manual parent spans.
  Groups auto-logged child spans under a descriptive label in the Traces UI.
- ``log_text()``: log a text artifact to the active run.
- ``log_dict()``: log a JSON artifact to the active run.
- ``log_token_usage()``: extract and log token counts from an OpenAI Agents
  SDK result object as metrics on the active run.

Usage::

    # Group multiple agent calls under one parent run
    with session_run("clinical_session", tags={"concern_count": "5"}):
        for concern in concerns:
            with agent_run("clinical_discussion", {"concern": concern}) as m:
                result = await Runner.run(agent, message)
                log_text(result.final_output, "discussion.txt")
                log_token_usage(result)
                m["output_chars"] = len(result.final_output)
            # latency_ms recorded automatically on agent_run exit
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import os
import re
import time
from contextlib import contextmanager
from typing import Any, Generator

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

_enabled: bool = False
_tracing_enabled: bool = False
_default_tags: dict[str, str] = {}


# ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


def _safe_filename(name: str) -> str:
    """Convert an arbitrary string to a safe artifact filename stem."""
    return re.sub(r"[^\w\-]", "_", name)


# ── Configuration ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


def configure(
    *,
    enabled: bool,
    tracking_uri: str,
    experiment_name: str,
    username: str = "",
    password: str = "",
    tracing_enabled: bool = True,
    default_tags: dict[str, str] | None = None,
) -> None:
    """Configure MLflow tracking and tracing. Called once at application startup.

    Controlled by two independent flags:

    - ``enabled`` (env: ``MLFLOW_ENABLED``): master switch for all MLflow
      features. When ``False``, nothing is initialised.
    - ``tracing_enabled`` (env: ``MLFLOW_TRACING_ENABLED``): controls
      distributed tracing only (``mlflow.openai.autolog()`` and
      ``trace_span()``).  Set to ``False`` to keep experiment tracking
      (runs / params / metrics) while skipping trace collection.

    ``default_tags`` are attached to the MLflow experiment and merged into
    every run/session opened by this process. Call-level tags take precedence
    on conflict. Typical usage: ``{"environment": "production"}``.

    When ``username`` and ``password`` are provided they are written to the
    ``MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME`` / ``MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD`` environment
    variables that the MLflow client reads for HTTP basic authentication.

    Args:
        enabled: Master switch — whether to enable any MLflow feature.
        tracking_uri: MLflow tracking server URI.
        experiment_name: Name of the MLflow experiment to log runs under.
        username: Basic auth username for the tracking server.
        password: Basic auth password for the tracking server.
        tracing_enabled: Whether to enable distributed tracing via
            ``mlflow.openai.autolog()`` and ``trace_span()``.
        default_tags: Key/value tags attached to every run and to the
            experiment itself. Useful for environment and version metadata.
    """
    global _enabled, _tracing_enabled, _default_tags

    # Assign default_tags before the early-return guard so a subsequent call
    # with enabled=True does not carry stale tags from a prior disabled call.
    _default_tags = default_tags or {}

    _enabled = enabled
    _tracing_enabled = enabled and tracing_enabled

    if not enabled:
        logger.debug("[mlflow] Tracking disabled")
        return

    try:
        import mlflow

        if username:
            os.environ["MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME"] = username
        if password:
            os.environ["MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD"] = password

        mlflow.set_tracking_uri(tracking_uri)
        mlflow.set_experiment(experiment_name)

        # Attach default tags to the experiment object (visible in the
        # Experiments UI, not just on individual runs).
        if _default_tags:
            mlflow.set_experiment_tags(_default_tags)

        if tracing_enabled:
            # Non-blocking async trace I/O — traces are flushed in a background
            # thread so agent streaming is not delayed by network round-trips.
            os.environ.setdefault("MLFLOW_ENABLE_ASYNC_TRACE_LOGGING", "true")

            # Auto-trace all OpenAI / Agents SDK calls: captures model, tokens,
            # latency, and full prompt/completion I/O as nested spans in the
            # MLflow Traces UI.  Works with Runner.run(), Runner.run_streamed(),
            # and direct openai.Client calls.
            mlflow.openai.autolog()

        logger.debug(
            "[mlflow] Tracking configured",
            extra={
                "tracking_uri": tracking_uri,
                "experiment": experiment_name,
                "auth": "basic" if username else "none",
                "tracing": tracing_enabled,
                "default_tags": list(_default_tags.keys()),
            },
        )
    except Exception:
        _enabled = False
        _tracing_enabled = False
        logger.warning("[mlflow] Failed to configure tracking — tracking disabled", exc_info=True)


# ── Session run (parent) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────


@contextmanager
def session_run(
    run_name: str,
    tags: dict[str, str] | None = None,
) -> Generator[None, None, None]:
    """Context manager that creates a parent MLflow run.

    Use this to group all ``agent_run`` child runs from one patient session
    under a single collapsible entry in the MLflow Experiments UI.  Each
    ``agent_run`` opened inside this block automatically detects the active
    parent and starts as a nested (child) run.

    When tracking is disabled or the server is unreachable, this is a silent
    no-op — all code inside the block runs normally.

    Args:
        run_name: Human-readable name for the parent run
                  (e.g., ``"clinical_session"``).
        tags: Optional extra tags merged on top of the default tags.

    Example::

        with session_run("clinical_session", tags={"num_concerns": "5"}):
            for concern in concerns:
                with agent_run("discussion", {"concern": concern}) as m:
                    result = await Runner.run(agent, message)
                    m["output_chars"] = len(result.final_output)
    """
    if not _enabled:
        yield
        return

    import mlflow  # deferred — only imported when tracking is active

    tracking_active = False
    merged_tags = {**_default_tags, **(tags or {})}
    try:
        mlflow.start_run(run_name=run_name, tags=merged_tags)
        tracking_active = True
    except Exception:
        logger.warning("[mlflow] Could not start session run '%s'", run_name, exc_info=True)

    try:
        yield
    finally:
        if tracking_active:
            try:
                mlflow.end_run()
            except Exception:
                logger.debug("[mlflow] Could not end session run '%s'", run_name)


# ── Agent run (child / standalone) ────────────────────────────────────────────


@contextmanager
def agent_run(
    run_name: str,
    params: dict[str, Any],
    tags: dict[str, str] | None = None,
) -> Generator[dict[str, float], None, None]:
    """Context manager for tracking an OpenAI agent invocation with MLflow.

    Logs ``params`` when the run starts and automatically records
    ``latency_ms`` on exit. The caller may add extra metrics to the yielded
    ``metrics`` dict before the context exits.

    When an active ``session_run`` is open, this run is started as a *nested*
    child run so both appear in the parent→child hierarchy in the UI. When
    used standalone, it starts as a top-level run.

    Default tags (set via ``configure(default_tags=...)``) are merged with
    call-level ``tags``; call-level tags take precedence on conflict.

    When tracking is disabled or the tracking server is unreachable, this is
    a silent no-op — the wrapped agent call proceeds normally.

    Args:
        run_name: Human-readable name for the MLflow run.
        params: Key/value pairs to log as MLflow parameters.
        tags: Optional extra tags merged on top of the default tags.

    Yields:
        A mutable ``metrics`` dict. Add float values to be logged on exit.
        ``latency_ms`` is set automatically and must not be overwritten.

    Example::

        with agent_run("clinical_chat", {"model": "gpt-4o"}) as metrics:
            result = await Runner.run(agent, prompt)
            metrics["output_chars"] = len(result.final_output)
    """
    metrics: dict[str, float] = {}
    start = time.perf_counter()

    if not _enabled:
        yield metrics
        return

    import mlflow  # deferred — only imported when tracking is active

    tracking_active = False
    merged_tags = {**_default_tags, **(tags or {})}
    try:
        # Nest under the active session_run when one is open; otherwise start
        # a standalone top-level run.  mlflow.active_run() uses contextvars
        # so it is safe across asyncio tasks created inside a session_run block.
        nested = mlflow.active_run() is not None
        mlflow.start_run(run_name=run_name, tags=merged_tags, nested=nested)
        mlflow.log_params({k: str(v)[:250] for k, v in params.items()})
        tracking_active = True
    except Exception:
        logger.warning("[mlflow] Could not start run '%s'", run_name, exc_info=True)

    try:
        yield metrics
    finally:
        duration_ms = round((time.perf_counter() - start) * 1000, 2)
        metrics.setdefault("latency_ms", duration_ms)

        if tracking_active:
            try:
                mlflow.log_metrics(metrics)
            except Exception:
                logger.debug("[mlflow] Could not log metrics for run '%s'", run_name)
            finally:
                try:
                    mlflow.end_run()
                except Exception:
                    logger.debug("[mlflow] Could not end run '%s'", run_name)


# ── Trace span (manual distributed tracing) ───────────────────────────────────


@contextmanager
def trace_span(
    name: str,
    attributes: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
) -> Generator[None, None, None]:
    """Context manager that creates a named MLflow trace span.

    Use this to create a parent span that groups the auto-logged child spans
    from ``mlflow.openai.autolog()`` under a descriptive label in the Traces
    UI.  When tracing is disabled or the server is unreachable, this is a
    silent no-op.

    Args:
        name: Span name shown in the MLflow Traces UI
              (e.g., ``"run_concerns_stream"``).
        attributes: Optional key/value pairs attached to the span as metadata
                    (e.g., ``{"concern": "Cardiac", "num_snps": "5"}``).

    Example::

        with trace_span("run_concerns_stream", {"num_messages": len(messages)}):
            result = Runner.run_streamed(agent, input=messages)
            async for event in result.stream_events():
                ...
    """
    if not _tracing_enabled:
        yield
        return

    import mlflow  # deferred — only imported when tracking is active

    try:
        with mlflow.start_span(name=name) as span:
            if attributes:
                span.set_attributes({k: str(v) for k, v in attributes.items()})
            yield
    except Exception:
        logger.debug("[mlflow] Could not create trace span '%s'", name)
        yield


# ── Artifact helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


def log_text(text: str, filename: str) -> None:
    """Log a plain-text artifact to the active MLflow run.

    Must be called while an active run is open (inside ``agent_run`` or
    ``session_run``).  Silently skips when tracking is disabled or the call
    fails for any reason.

    Args:
        text: The text content to store.
        filename: Artifact path relative to the run's artifact root
                  (e.g., ``"discussion_Cardiac.txt"``).
    """
    if not _enabled:
        return
    try:
        import mlflow

        mlflow.log_text(text, filename)
    except Exception:
        logger.debug("[mlflow] Could not log text artifact '%s'", filename)


def log_dict(data: dict[str, Any], filename: str) -> None:
    """Log a JSON-serialisable dict as an artifact to the active MLflow run.

    Must be called while an active run is open.  Silently skips when tracking
    is disabled or the call fails for any reason.

    Args:
        data: JSON-serialisable dict to store.
        filename: Artifact path relative to the run's artifact root
                  (e.g., ``"extracted_concerns.json"``).
    """
    if not _enabled:
        return
    try:
        import mlflow

        mlflow.log_dict(data, filename)
    except Exception:
        logger.debug("[mlflow] Could not log dict artifact '%s'", filename)


# ── Token usage ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


def log_token_usage(result: Any) -> None:
    """Extract and log token counts from an OpenAI Agents SDK result object.

    Reads ``result.raw_responses``, sums ``usage.input_tokens`` and
    ``usage.output_tokens`` across all responses, and logs them as
    ``input_tokens``, ``output_tokens``, and ``total_tokens`` metrics on the
    currently active MLflow run.

    For streaming results (``RunResultStreaming``), call this **after** the
    ``stream_events()`` iterator is exhausted — ``raw_responses`` is only
    fully populated once all events have been consumed.

    Must be called inside an active run (``agent_run`` or ``session_run``).
    Silently no-ops when tracking is disabled, when no usage data is found,
    or on any failure.

    Args:
        result: A ``RunResult`` or ``RunResultStreaming`` from the OpenAI
                Agents SDK ``Runner``.
    """
    if not _enabled:
        return
    try:
        import mlflow

        raw_responses = getattr(result, "raw_responses", None)
        if not raw_responses:
            return

        input_tokens = 0
        output_tokens = 0
        for resp in raw_responses:
            usage = getattr(resp, "usage", None)
            if usage:
                input_tokens += getattr(usage, "input_tokens", 0) or 0
                output_tokens += getattr(usage, "output_tokens", 0) or 0

        total_tokens = input_tokens + output_tokens
        if total_tokens == 0:
            return

        mlflow.log_metrics(
            {
                "input_tokens": float(input_tokens),
                "output_tokens": float(output_tokens),
                "total_tokens": float(total_tokens),
            }
        )
    except Exception:
        logger.debug("[mlflow] Could not log token usage")

  MLflow *child* run (params + metrics). Automatically nests under an active
  ``session_run``; starts as a top-level run when used standalone. Degrades
  gracefully to a no-op when MLflow is disabled or unreachable.
- ``trace_span()``: lightweight context manager for manual parent spans.
  Groups auto-logged child spans under a descriptive label in the Traces UI.
- ``log_text()``: log a text artifact to the active run.
- ``log_dict()``: log a JSON artifact to the active run.
- ``log_token_usage()``: extract and log token counts from an OpenAI Agents
  SDK result object as metrics on the active run.

Usage::

    # Group multiple agent calls under one parent run
    with session_run("clinical_session", tags={"concern_count": "5"}):
        for concern in concerns:
            with agent_run("clinical_discussion", {"concern": concern}) as m:
                result = await Runner.run(agent, message)
                log_text(result.final_output, "discussion.txt")
                log_token_usage(result)
                m["output_chars"] = len(result.final_output)
            # latency_ms recorded automatically on agent_run exit
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import os
import re
import time
from contextlib import contextmanager
from typing import Any, Generator

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

_enabled: bool = False
_tracing_enabled: bool = False
_default_tags: dict[str, str] = {}


# ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


def _safe_filename(name: str) -> str:
    """Convert an arbitrary string to a safe artifact filename stem."""
    return re.sub(r"[^\w\-]", "_", name)


# ── Configuration ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


def configure(
    *,
    enabled: bool,
    tracking_uri: str,
    experiment_name: str,
    username: str = "",
    password: str = "",
    tracing_enabled: bool = True,
    default_tags: dict[str, str] | None = None,
) -> None:
    """Configure MLflow tracking and tracing. Called once at application startup.

    Controlled by two independent flags:

    - ``enabled`` (env: ``MLFLOW_ENABLED``): master switch for all MLflow
      features. When ``False``, nothing is initialised.
    - ``tracing_enabled`` (env: ``MLFLOW_TRACING_ENABLED``): controls
      distributed tracing only (``mlflow.openai.autolog()`` and
      ``trace_span()``).  Set to ``False`` to keep experiment tracking
      (runs / params / metrics) while skipping trace collection.

    ``default_tags`` are attached to the MLflow experiment and merged into
    every run/session opened by this process. Call-level tags take precedence
    on conflict. Typical usage: ``{"environment": "production"}``.

    When ``username`` and ``password`` are provided they are written to the
    ``MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME`` / ``MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD`` environment
    variables that the MLflow client reads for HTTP basic authentication.

    Args:
        enabled: Master switch — whether to enable any MLflow feature.
        tracking_uri: MLflow tracking server URI.
        experiment_name: Name of the MLflow experiment to log runs under.
        username: Basic auth username for the tracking server.
        password: Basic auth password for the tracking server.
        tracing_enabled: Whether to enable distributed tracing via
            ``mlflow.openai.autolog()`` and ``trace_span()``.
        default_tags: Key/value tags attached to every run and to the
            experiment itself. Useful for environment and version metadata.
    """
    global _enabled, _tracing_enabled, _default_tags

    # Assign default_tags before the early-return guard so a subsequent call
    # with enabled=True does not carry stale tags from a prior disabled call.
    _default_tags = default_tags or {}

    _enabled = enabled
    _tracing_enabled = enabled and tracing_enabled

    if not enabled:
        logger.debug("[mlflow] Tracking disabled")
        return

    try:
        import mlflow

        if username:
            os.environ["MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME"] = username
        if password:
            os.environ["MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD"] = password

        mlflow.set_tracking_uri(tracking_uri)
        mlflow.set_experiment(experiment_name)

        # Attach default tags to the experiment object (visible in the
        # Experiments UI, not just on individual runs).
        if _default_tags:
            mlflow.set_experiment_tags(_default_tags)

        if tracing_enabled:
            # Non-blocking async trace I/O — traces are flushed in a background
            # thread so agent streaming is not delayed by network round-trips.
            os.environ.setdefault("MLFLOW_ENABLE_ASYNC_TRACE_LOGGING", "true")

            # Auto-trace all OpenAI / Agents SDK calls: captures model, tokens,
            # latency, and full prompt/completion I/O as nested spans in the
            # MLflow Traces UI.  Works with Runner.run(), Runner.run_streamed(),
            # and direct openai.Client calls.
            mlflow.openai.autolog()

        logger.debug(
            "[mlflow] Tracking configured",
            extra={
                "tracking_uri": tracking_uri,
                "experiment": experiment_name,
                "auth": "basic" if username else "none",
                "tracing": tracing_enabled,
                "default_tags": list(_default_tags.keys()),
            },
        )
    except Exception:
        _enabled = False
        _tracing_enabled = False
        logger.warning("[mlflow] Failed to configure tracking — tracking disabled", exc_info=True)


# ── Session run (parent) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────


@contextmanager
def session_run(
    run_name: str,
    tags: dict[str, str] | None = None,
) -> Generator[None, None, None]:
    """Context manager that creates a parent MLflow run.

    Use this to group all ``agent_run`` child runs from one patient session
    under a single collapsible entry in the MLflow Experiments UI.  Each
    ``agent_run`` opened inside this block automatically detects the active
    parent and starts as a nested (child) run.

    When tracking is disabled or the server is unreachable, this is a silent
    no-op — all code inside the block runs normally.

    Args:
        run_name: Human-readable name for the parent run
                  (e.g., ``"clinical_session"``).
        tags: Optional extra tags merged on top of the default tags.

    Example::

        with session_run("clinical_session", tags={"num_concerns": "5"}):
            for concern in concerns:
                with agent_run("discussion", {"concern": concern}) as m:
                    result = await Runner.run(agent, message)
                    m["output_chars"] = len(result.final_output)
    """
    if not _enabled:
        yield
        return

    import mlflow  # deferred — only imported when tracking is active

    tracking_active = False
    merged_tags = {**_default_tags, **(tags or {})}
    try:
        mlflow.start_run(run_name=run_name, tags=merged_tags)
        tracking_active = True
    except Exception:
        logger.warning("[mlflow] Could not start session run '%s'", run_name, exc_info=True)

    try:
        yield
    finally:
        if tracking_active:
            try:
                mlflow.end_run()
            except Exception:
                logger.debug("[mlflow] Could not end session run '%s'", run_name)


# ── Agent run (child / standalone) ────────────────────────────────────────────


@contextmanager
def agent_run(
    run_name: str,
    params: dict[str, Any],
    tags: dict[str, str] | None = None,
) -> Generator[dict[str, float], None, None]:
    """Context manager for tracking an OpenAI agent invocation with MLflow.

    Logs ``params`` when the run starts and automatically records
    ``latency_ms`` on exit. The caller may add extra metrics to the yielded
    ``metrics`` dict before the context exits.

    When an active ``session_run`` is open, this run is started as a *nested*
    child run so both appear in the parent→child hierarchy in the UI. When
    used standalone, it starts as a top-level run.

    Default tags (set via ``configure(default_tags=...)``) are merged with
    call-level ``tags``; call-level tags take precedence on conflict.

    When tracking is disabled or the tracking server is unreachable, this is
    a silent no-op — the wrapped agent call proceeds normally.

    Args:
        run_name: Human-readable name for the MLflow run.
        params: Key/value pairs to log as MLflow parameters.
        tags: Optional extra tags merged on top of the default tags.

    Yields:
        A mutable ``metrics`` dict. Add float values to be logged on exit.
        ``latency_ms`` is set automatically and must not be overwritten.

    Example::

        with agent_run("clinical_chat", {"model": "gpt-4o"}) as metrics:
            result = await Runner.run(agent, prompt)
            metrics["output_chars"] = len(result.final_output)
    """
    metrics: dict[str, float] = {}
    start = time.perf_counter()

    if not _enabled:
        yield metrics
        return

    import mlflow  # deferred — only imported when tracking is active

    tracking_active = False
    merged_tags = {**_default_tags, **(tags or {})}
    try:
        # Nest under the active session_run when one is open; otherwise start
        # a standalone top-level run.  mlflow.active_run() uses contextvars
        # so it is safe across asyncio tasks created inside a session_run block.
        nested = mlflow.active_run() is not None
        mlflow.start_run(run_name=run_name, tags=merged_tags, nested=nested)
        mlflow.log_params({k: str(v)[:250] for k, v in params.items()})
        tracking_active = True
    except Exception:
        logger.warning("[mlflow] Could not start run '%s'", run_name, exc_info=True)

    try:
        yield metrics
    finally:
        duration_ms = round((time.perf_counter() - start) * 1000, 2)
        metrics.setdefault("latency_ms", duration_ms)

        if tracking_active:
            try:
                mlflow.log_metrics(metrics)
            except Exception:
                logger.debug("[mlflow] Could not log metrics for run '%s'", run_name)
            finally:
                try:
                    mlflow.end_run()
                except Exception:
                    logger.debug("[mlflow] Could not end run '%s'", run_name)


# ── Trace span (manual distributed tracing) ───────────────────────────────────


@contextmanager
def trace_span(
    name: str,
    attributes: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
) -> Generator[None, None, None]:
    """Context manager that creates a named MLflow trace span.

    Use this to create a parent span that groups the auto-logged child spans
    from ``mlflow.openai.autolog()`` under a descriptive label in the Traces
    UI.  When tracing is disabled or the server is unreachable, this is a
    silent no-op.

    Args:
        name: Span name shown in the MLflow Traces UI
              (e.g., ``"run_concerns_stream"``).
        attributes: Optional key/value pairs attached to the span as metadata
                    (e.g., ``{"concern": "Cardiac", "num_snps": "5"}``).

    Example::

        with trace_span("run_concerns_stream", {"num_messages": len(messages)}):
            result = Runner.run_streamed(agent, input=messages)
            async for event in result.stream_events():
                ...
    """
    if not _tracing_enabled:
        yield
        return

    import mlflow  # deferred — only imported when tracking is active

    try:
        with mlflow.start_span(name=name) as span:
            if attributes:
                span.set_attributes({k: str(v) for k, v in attributes.items()})
            yield
    except Exception:
        logger.debug("[mlflow] Could not create trace span '%s'", name)
        yield


# ── Artifact helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


def log_text(text: str, filename: str) -> None:
    """Log a plain-text artifact to the active MLflow run.

    Must be called while an active run is open (inside ``agent_run`` or
    ``session_run``).  Silently skips when tracking is disabled or the call
    fails for any reason.

    Args:
        text: The text content to store.
        filename: Artifact path relative to the run's artifact root
                  (e.g., ``"discussion_Cardiac.txt"``).
    """
    if not _enabled:
        return
    try:
        import mlflow

        mlflow.log_text(text, filename)
    except Exception:
        logger.debug("[mlflow] Could not log text artifact '%s'", filename)


def log_dict(data: dict[str, Any], filename: str) -> None:
    """Log a JSON-serialisable dict as an artifact to the active MLflow run.

    Must be called while an active run is open.  Silently skips when tracking
    is disabled or the call fails for any reason.

    Args:
        data: JSON-serialisable dict to store.
        filename: Artifact path relative to the run's artifact root
                  (e.g., ``"extracted_concerns.json"``).
    """
    if not _enabled:
        return
    try:
        import mlflow

        mlflow.log_dict(data, filename)
    except Exception:
        logger.debug("[mlflow] Could not log dict artifact '%s'", filename)


# ── Token usage ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


def log_token_usage(result: Any) -> None:
    """Extract and log token counts from an OpenAI Agents SDK result object.

    Reads ``result.raw_responses``, sums ``usage.input_tokens`` and
    ``usage.output_tokens`` across all responses, and logs them as
    ``input_tokens``, ``output_tokens``, and ``total_tokens`` metrics on the
    currently active MLflow run.

    For streaming results (``RunResultStreaming``), call this **after** the
    ``stream_events()`` iterator is exhausted — ``raw_responses`` is only
    fully populated once all events have been consumed.

    Must be called inside an active run (``agent_run`` or ``session_run``).
    Silently no-ops when tracking is disabled, when no usage data is found,
    or on any failure.

    Args:
        result: A ``RunResult`` or ``RunResultStreaming`` from the OpenAI
                Agents SDK ``Runner``.
    """
    if not _enabled:
        return
    try:
        import mlflow

        raw_responses = getattr(result, "raw_responses", None)
        if not raw_responses:
            return

        input_tokens = 0
        output_tokens = 0
        for resp in raw_responses:
            usage = getattr(resp, "usage", None)
            if usage:
                input_tokens += getattr(usage, "input_tokens", 0) or 0
                output_tokens += getattr(usage, "output_tokens", 0) or 0

        total_tokens = input_tokens + output_tokens
        if total_tokens == 0:
            return

        mlflow.log_metrics(
            {
                "input_tokens": float(input_tokens),
                "output_tokens": float(output_tokens),
                "total_tokens": float(total_tokens),
            }
        )
    except Exception:
        logger.debug("[mlflow] Could not log token usage")

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""MLflow tracking and tracing utilities for OpenAI agent calls.

Provides:
- ``configure()``: one-time setup that enables experiment tracking AND
  auto-tracing via ``mlflow.openai.autolog()``. Auto-tracing captures every
  OpenAI / Agents SDK call as a trace span (tokens, latency, model I/O) with
  zero per-call instrumentation.
- ``agent_run()``: context manager that wraps an agent invocation with an
  MLflow *run* (structured params/metrics). Degrades gracefully to a no-op
  when MLflow is disabled or the tracking server is unreachable.
- ``trace_span()``: lightweight context manager for manual parent spans.
  Wrap a block that makes one or more OpenAI calls to group the auto-logged
  child spans under a descriptive parent span in the Traces UI.

Usage::

    # Experiment tracking (params + metrics)
    with agent_run("clinical_discussion", {"model": "gpt-4o", "concern": name}) as metrics:
        result = await Runner.run(agent, message)
        metrics["output_chars"] = len(result.final_output)
    # latency_ms is recorded automatically on exit

    # Manual parent span (trace view)
    with trace_span("generate_discussion", {"concern": name, "num_snps": n}):
        result = await Runner.run(agent, message)
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import os
import time
from contextlib import contextmanager
from typing import Any, Generator

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

_enabled: bool = False
_tracing_enabled: bool = False


def configure(
    *,
    enabled: bool,
    tracking_uri: str,
    experiment_name: str,
    username: str = "",
    password: str = "",
    tracing_enabled: bool = True,
) -> None:
    """Configure MLflow tracking and tracing. Called once at application startup.

    Controlled by two independent flags:

    - ``enabled`` (env: ``MLFLOW_ENABLED``): master switch for all MLflow
      features. When ``False``, nothing is initialised.
    - ``tracing_enabled`` (env: ``MLFLOW_TRACING_ENABLED``): controls
      distributed tracing only (``mlflow.openai.autolog()`` and
      ``trace_span()``).  Set to ``False`` to keep experiment tracking
      (runs / params / metrics) while skipping trace collection.

    When ``username`` and ``password`` are provided they are written to the
    ``MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME`` / ``MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD`` environment
    variables that the MLflow client reads for HTTP basic authentication.

    Args:
        enabled: Master switch — whether to enable any MLflow feature.
        tracking_uri: MLflow tracking server URI.
        experiment_name: Name of the MLflow experiment to log runs under.
        username: Basic auth username for the tracking server.
        password: Basic auth password for the tracking server.
        tracing_enabled: Whether to enable distributed tracing via
            ``mlflow.openai.autolog()`` and ``trace_span()``.
    """
    global _enabled, _tracing_enabled
    _enabled = enabled
    _tracing_enabled = enabled and tracing_enabled

    if not enabled:
        logger.debug("[mlflow] Tracking disabled")
        return

    try:
        import mlflow

        if username:
            os.environ["MLFLOW_TRACKING_USERNAME"] = username
        if password:
            os.environ["MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD"] = password

        mlflow.set_tracking_uri(tracking_uri)
        mlflow.set_experiment(experiment_name)

        if tracing_enabled:
            # Non-blocking async trace I/O — traces are flushed in a background
            # thread so agent streaming is not delayed by network round-trips.
            os.environ.setdefault("MLFLOW_ENABLE_ASYNC_TRACE_LOGGING", "true")

            # Auto-trace all OpenAI / Agents SDK calls: captures model, tokens,
            # latency, and full prompt/completion I/O as nested spans in the
            # MLflow Traces UI.  Works with Runner.run(), Runner.run_streamed(),
            # and direct openai.Client calls.
            mlflow.openai.autolog()

        logger.debug(
            "[mlflow] Tracking configured",
            extra={
                "tracking_uri": tracking_uri,
                "experiment": experiment_name,
                "auth": "basic" if username else "none",
                "tracing": tracing_enabled,
            },
        )
    except Exception:
        _enabled = False
        _tracing_enabled = False
        logger.warning("[mlflow] Failed to configure tracking — tracking disabled", exc_info=True)


@contextmanager
def agent_run(
    run_name: str,
    params: dict[str, Any],
    tags: dict[str, str] | None = None,
) -> Generator[dict[str, float], None, None]:
    """Context manager for tracking an OpenAI agent run with MLflow.

    Logs ``params`` when the run starts and automatically records
    ``latency_ms`` on exit. The caller may add extra metrics to the
    yielded ``metrics`` dict before the context exits.

    When tracking is disabled or the tracking server is unreachable, this
    is a silent no-op — the wrapped agent call proceeds normally.

    Args:
        run_name: Human-readable name for the MLflow run.
        params: Key/value pairs to log as MLflow parameters.
        tags: Optional key/value string tags for the run.

    Yields:
        A mutable ``metrics`` dict. Add float values to be logged on exit.
        ``latency_ms`` is set automatically and must not be overwritten.

    Example::

        with agent_run("clinical_chat", {"model": "gpt-4o"}) as metrics:
            result = await Runner.run(agent, prompt)
            metrics["output_chars"] = len(result.final_output)
    """
    metrics: dict[str, float] = {}
    start = time.perf_counter()

    if not _enabled:
        yield metrics
        return

    import mlflow  # deferred — only imported when tracking is active

    tracking_active = False
    try:
        mlflow.start_run(run_name=run_name, tags=tags or {})
        mlflow.log_params({k: str(v)[:250] for k, v in params.items()})
        tracking_active = True
    except Exception:
        logger.warning("[mlflow] Could not start run '%s'", run_name, exc_info=True)

    try:
        yield metrics
    finally:
        duration_ms = round((time.perf_counter() - start) * 1000, 2)
        metrics.setdefault("latency_ms", duration_ms)

        if tracking_active:
            try:
                mlflow.log_metrics(metrics)
            except Exception:
                logger.debug("[mlflow] Could not log metrics for run '%s'", run_name)
            finally:
                try:
                    mlflow.end_run()
                except Exception:
                    logger.debug("[mlflow] Could not end run '%s'", run_name)


@contextmanager
def trace_span(
    name: str,
    attributes: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
) -> Generator[None, None, None]:
    """Context manager that creates a named MLflow trace span.

    Use this to create a parent span that groups the auto-logged child spans
    from ``mlflow.openai.autolog()`` under a descriptive label in the Traces
    UI.  When tracking is disabled or the server is unreachable, this is a
    silent no-op.

    Args:
        name: Span name shown in the MLflow Traces UI
              (e.g., ``"run_concerns_stream"``).
        attributes: Optional key/value pairs attached to the span as metadata
                    (e.g., ``{"concern": "Cardiac", "num_snps": 5}``).

    Example::

        with trace_span("run_concerns_stream", {"num_messages": len(messages)}):
            result = Runner.run_streamed(agent, input=messages)
            async for event in result.stream_events():
                ...
    """
    if not _tracing_enabled:
        yield
        return

    import mlflow  # deferred — only imported when tracking is active

    try:
        with mlflow.start_span(name=name) as span:
            if attributes:
                span.set_attributes({k: str(v) for k, v in attributes.items()})
            yield
    except Exception:
        logger.debug("[mlflow] Could not create trace span '%s'", name)
        yield

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""FastAPI application entry point and lifespan management."""

import logging
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager

import sentry_sdk
from fastapi import FastAPI, Request, status
from fastapi.exceptions import RequestValidationError
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse

from app.ai.ai_data_service import AIDataService
from app.ai.cache_refresh_task import CacheRefreshTask
from app.ai.mlflow_tracker import configure as configure_mlflow
from app.ai.reference_data_cache import ReferenceDataCache
from app.core.config import get_settings
from app.core.logging_config import setup_logging
from app.db import PostgresDatabase, set_postgres_instance
from app.dependencies import set_ai_data_service, set_reference_data_cache
from app.middleware import RequestLoggingMiddleware, SwaggerAuthMiddleware
from app.repository import init_repository
from app.routes import ai_router, data_router, misc_router

settings = get_settings()
setup_logging(
    environment=settings.general.environment.value,
    debug=settings.general.debug,
)
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# Sentry must be initialized before the FastAPI app is created so the
# automatic FastAPI integration (tracing, error capture) is active from start.
if settings.sentry.sentry_dsn:
    sentry_sdk.init(
        dsn=settings.sentry.sentry_dsn,
        environment=settings.general.environment.value,
        traces_sample_rate=settings.sentry.sentry_traces_sample_rate,
        profiles_sample_rate=settings.sentry.sentry_profiles_sample_rate,
        send_default_pii=True,
    )
    logger.info(
        "Sentry initialized",
        extra={"environment": settings.general.environment.value},
    )
else:
    logger.warning("Sentry DSN not configured — error tracking disabled")


@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    """Manage application startup and shutdown lifecycle.

    Args:
        app: The FastAPI application instance.
    """
    logger.info(f"Initializing application in environment: {settings.general.environment}")

    configure_mlflow(
        enabled=settings.mlflow.mlflow_enabled,
        tracing_enabled=settings.mlflow.mlflow_tracing_enabled,
        tracking_uri=settings.mlflow.mlflow_tracking_uri,
        experiment_name=settings.mlflow.mlflow_experiment_name,
        username=settings.mlflow.mlflow_username,
        password=settings.mlflow.mlflow_password,
    )

    try:
        pg_database = PostgresDatabase()
        await pg_database.connect()
        set_postgres_instance(pg_database)

        if await pg_database.health_check():
            logger.info("Database initialized successfully")
    except Exception as e:
        sentry_sdk.capture_exception(e)
        logger.error(f"Error initializing database: {e}")
        raise

    init_repository(pg_db=pg_database)

    # Load R&D reference data cache and initialize AI Data Service
    cache = ReferenceDataCache(db=pg_database)
    try:
        await cache.ensure_loaded()
        set_reference_data_cache(cache)
        ai_data_service = AIDataService(db=pg_database)
        set_ai_data_service(instance=ai_data_service)
        logger.info("AI Data Service initialized and reference data loaded")
    except Exception as e:
        sentry_sdk.capture_exception(e)
        logger.error(f"Error initializing AI Data Service: {e}")
        raise

    # Start periodic cache refresh (every 5 minutes)
    refresh_task = CacheRefreshTask(cache=cache, interval_seconds=300)
    refresh_task.start()

    yield

    await refresh_task.stop()

    try:
        await pg_database.disconnect()
        logger.info("Database disconnected successfully")
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Error disconnecting database: {e}")

    logger.info("Shutting down application.")


app = FastAPI(
    title="IntelxDNA SNP's API",
    description=("Documentation for [IntelxDNA SNP's API]"),
    version="v0.0.0",
    lifespan=lifespan,
    openapi_url="/lexxi/openapi.json" if settings.general.environment == "development" else None,
    docs_url="/docs" if settings.general.environment == "development" else None,
    redoc_url="/redoc" if settings.general.environment == "development" else None,
)

app.add_middleware(middleware_class=SwaggerAuthMiddleware)
app.add_middleware(
    middleware_class=CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=settings.cors.cors_allow_origins,
    allow_credentials=settings.cors.cors_allow_credentials,
    allow_methods=settings.cors.cors_allow_methods,
    allow_headers=settings.cors.cors_allow_headers,
    expose_headers=settings.cors.cors_expose_headers,
    max_age=settings.cors.cors_max_age,
)
# Must be added last so it wraps all other middleware
app.add_middleware(middleware_class=RequestLoggingMiddleware)

app.include_router(router=misc_router)
app.include_router(router=data_router)
app.include_router(router=ai_router)


@app.exception_handler(RequestValidationError)
async def _validation_exception_handler(
    request: Request, exc: RequestValidationError
) -> JSONResponse:
    """Log request body/query validation failures (422) with field-level details."""
    logger.warning(
        "Request validation failed",
        extra={
            "http_method": request.method,
            "http_path": request.url.path,
            "client_ip": request.client.host if request.client else "unknown",
            "validation_errors": exc.errors(),
        },
    )
    return JSONResponse(
        status_code=status.HTTP_422_UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY,
        content={"detail": exc.errors()},
    )


@app.exception_handler(Exception)
async def _unhandled_exception_handler(request: Request, exc: Exception) -> JSONResponse:
    """Log and return 500 for any exception not handled by a route or other handler."""
    logger.exception(
        "Unhandled server error",
        extra={
            "http_method": request.method,
            "http_path": request.url.path,
            "client_ip": request.client.host if request.client else "unknown",
            "exception_type": type(exc).__name__,
        },
    )
    return JSONResponse(
        status_code=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
        content={"detail": "Internal server error"},
    )


# Sentry verification endpoint — non-production only.
# Hit GET /sentry-debug to trigger a deliberate ZeroDivisionError and confirm
# that the event appears in your Sentry project within a few seconds.
if settings.general.environment.value != "production":

    @app.get("/sentry-debug", tags=["Debug"], include_in_schema=False)
    async def sentry_debug() -> None:
        """Trigger a deliberate error to verify Sentry is receiving events."""
        _ = 1 / 0
